brief_excerpt: Impact of AI-driven automation on US labor markets in 2026: which sectors are being disrupted, current employment data trends, wage dynamics, policy responses from the administration and Fed, and investment implications for labor-intensive industries.

key_sources.title: BLS Occupational Employment and Wage Statistics 2024
key_sources.summary: Source for occupational wage benchmarks: RN $89K, IT Support $62K, AI/ML Engineer $164K median annual wages.

key_sources.title: Federal Reserve Distributional Financial Accounts (DFA)
key_sources.summary: Documents that top 10% of US households own approximately 87% of all equities; bottom 50% own approximately 0.5%.

key_sources.title: Federal Reserve FOMC Statement June 2025
key_sources.summary: FOMC cut rates 25bps to 4.125%, citing explicit labor market softening language as policy driver.

key_sources.title: HHS / BLS Healthcare Workforce Projections 2024-2030
key_sources.summary: Projects 1.8 million healthcare worker shortage by 2026-2028; used to benchmark DOGE retraining pipeline contribution (2.5% gap fill).

key_sources.title: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna — AI Hiring Freeze Statement
key_sources.summary: IBM CEO stated plan to pause hiring in ~7,800 back-office roles expected to be replaced by AI over 5 years.

key_sources.title: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna — AI Job Replacement Announcement
key_sources.summary: IBM CEO confirmed 7,800 back-office roles to be replaced by AI over 5 years; hiring freeze announced.

key_sources.title: IBM WatsonX Bookings — CEO Arvind Krishna Earnings Call Statements
key_sources.summary: IBM CEO Krishna cited $1B+ in WatsonX bookings in 2024 earnings calls; WatsonX revenue not separately disclosed; embedded in Software segment.

key_sources.title: OPM Federal Employee Pay Statistics 2024
key_sources.summary: Source for $72,000 median federal civilian salary used in retraining opportunity cost calculations.

key_sources.title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff — AI Over Headcount
key_sources.summary: Salesforce cut 8,000 jobs; Benioff cited AI enabling more output with fewer workers as core justification.

key_sources.title: UPS 12,000 Job Cuts — March 2025 Announcement
key_sources.summary: UPS announced elimination of 12,000 positions in March 2025, explicitly citing logistics automation investments.

key_findings.statement: Nvidia stock returned +507% since January 2022, reaching $182.54 in March 2026.
AMN Healthcare stock declined −86.0% from its November 2022 peak of $127.04, driven by AI clinical workflow automation.
Robert Half International declined −78.3% from its February 2022 peak, reflecting structural AI displacement of staffing intermediaries.
ManpowerGroup declined −74.5% from its September 2021 peak.
Vornado Realty Trust (VNO) declined −35.6% and BXP declined −21.9% since the February 2025 DOGE announcement, providing a REIT proxy signal for DC-area commercial real estate stress.
Nvidia (NVDA) total return from January 2022 to June 2025 was +425.5%, making it the top-performing AI-era equity in the tracked set.
ManpowerGroup (MAN) returned -53.4% and Robert Half (RHI) returned -58.6% over the same period, reflecting structural AI disruption to the staffing industry.
IBM announced 7,800 back-office job replacements by AI in May 2023; CEO Arvind Krishna specifically cited AI as the replacement rationale.
VIX current reading of 15.78 is 11.7% below its 25-year historical median of 17.88, despite 186,600+ documented AI-automation layoffs.
Nvidia (NVDA) returned +1,047% since January 2023; AMN Healthcare returned -79% over the same period — a 1,126 percentage point divergence.

open_questions.question: Has IBM provided any breakout of WatsonX revenue in its Software segment filings, analyst days, or supplemental disclosures in H1 2025 that would allow verification of bookings-to-revenue conversion rate?
open_questions.note: The entire IBM investment thesis hinges on WatsonX conversion. The +106pp narrative premium will collapse or be justified based on the next 2 quarters of Software segment growth.

open_questions.question: Do IBM, Salesforce, or Workday 2024-2025 earnings call transcripts explicitly state 'not backfilling' junior roles as opposed to general hiring caution, allowing cleaner AI-substitution attribution?
open_questions.note: Cleanest evidence for Agenda A_c9c089e2 demand-side attribution. Seek IR transcripts or 10-K language distinguishing AI substitution from cyclical hiring restraint.

open_questions.question: Did VNO's −35.6% post-DOGE decline reflect pre-existing leverage/refinancing stress, or is it cleanly attributable to federal tenant demand destruction?
open_questions.note: Critical for Agenda A_d67a2ddb — need to know if REIT proxy is contaminated by idiosyncratic VNO balance sheet risk (VNO had ~$8B debt pre-2025). Seek VNO 2025 annual report or analyst notes.

open_questions.question: Were the June and September 2025 Fed cuts officially characterized as 'labor market' cuts in FOMC minutes, or as broader 'economic softening' cuts?
open_questions.note: If FOMC minutes explicitly name AI displacement or labor market structural shifts, that is tier-1 confirmation of the monetary policy response thesis. Search FOMC transcripts.

open_questions.question: What is the DOGE legal status as of Q1 2026 — have federal courts blocked any significant portion of the 100–150k eliminations, and if so, how many positions were actually net-eliminated vs placed on paid administrative leave?
open_questions.note: Gross vs net eliminations matters for the DC REIT thesis and for federal payroll demand signal. Court injunctions may mean actual net displacement is lower than 150k.

open_questions.question: What is the specific sequence of AI transcription technology adoption in US hospitals (Nuance/DAX Copilot, ambient AI documentation) and was there a specific contract or product milestone in mid-2023 that triggered AMN Healthcare's equity step-change decline?
open_questions.note: AMN equity shows step-change in mid-2023 that correlates with AI documentation tool rollouts; needs sourced narrative confirmation.

open_questions.question: Why is Leidos stock +61.4% from Jan 2023 despite -3.6% revenue contraction — which specific DoD/intelligence AI modernization contracts explain this valuation divergence from BAH?
open_questions.note: LDOS vs BAH divergence is the key DOGE paradox: some contractors winning AI replacement contracts while others lose headcount-based contracts. Needs program-level identification.

open_questions.question: What specific policy mechanism triggered the April 2025 VIX spike to 52.3 and its rapid normalization? Was it tariff pause announcement or Fed emergency language?
open_questions.note: VIX data confirms 52.3 max but the narrative resolution mechanism needs qual sourcing — this is the one moment equity holders felt labor-style distress.

open_questions.question: Has the current administration proposed any federal workforce retraining or AI transition assistance program, or is the policy response (DOGE cuts + rate cuts) entirely labor supply-reducing rather than skills-augmenting?
open_questions.note: Critical for policy section — if the answer is 'no retraining programs,' that is itself a policy finding that sharpens the narrative.
